content:
# Oil Isothermal Compressibility

**Summary**: The change in volume of oil per unit change in pressure at a constant temperature.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1990-al-jarri|Al-Jarri, 1990]] developed an oil isothermal compressibility correlation for values at and below the bubblepoint pressure.

## Scientific Context

- [[1990-al-jarri|Al-Jarri, 1990]] Crucial for calculating reservoir volumes and production rates.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The new correlation showed an average absolute percent relative error of 3.49%. ([[1990-al-jarri|Al-Jarri, 1990, p. 91]])
- [[1990-al-jarri|Al-Jarri, 1990]] 0.0 to 0.030 (1/psi)
